heading: Promotion policy objectives for joint officers
label: 662 Promotion policy objectives for joint officers
notes: Amendments2014— struck out subsec. (a) designation and heading “Qualifications.—” before “The Secretary of Defense” and struck out subsec. (b) which related to annual report.Pub. L. 113–2912009—Subsec. (a)(2). made technical amendment to directory language of . See 2008 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 111–84Pub. L. 110–417, § 523(1)2008—Subsec. (a)(2). , as amended by , substituted “officers in the grade of major (or in the case of the Navy, lieutenant commander) or above who have been designated as a joint qualified officer” for “officers who are serving in or have served in joint duty assignments”.Pub. L. 110–417, § 523(1)Pub. L. 111–84Subsec. (b). , inserted “or on the Joint Staff, and officers who have been designated as a joint qualified officer in the grades of major (or in the case of the Navy, lieutenant commander) through colonel (or in the case of the Navy, captain)” after “joint duty assignments”.Pub. L. 110–417, § 523(2)substituted “paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (a)” for “paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) of subsection (a)”.Pub. L. 110–1812006—Subsec. (a). inserted “and” at end of par. (1), added par. (2), and struck out former pars. (2) and (3) which read as follows:Pub. L. 109–364“(2) officers who have the joint specialty are expected, as a group, to be promoted—“(A) during the period beginning on , and ending on , at a rate not less than the rate for officers of the same armed force in the same grade and competitive category; andDecember 28, 2001December 27, 2006“(B) after , at a rate not less than the rate for officers of the same armed force in the same grade and competitive category who are serving on, or have served on, the headquarters staff of their armed force; andDecember 27, 2006“(3) officers who are serving in, or have served in, joint duty assignments (other than officers covered in paragraphs (1) and (2)) are expected, as a group, to be promoted to the next higher grade at a rate not less than the rate for all officers of the same armed force in the same grade and competitive category.”2004—Subsec. (a)(2). substituted “” for “” in two places.Pub. L. 108–375December 27, 2006December 27, 20042002—Subsec. (a)(2)(A). , substituted “during the period beginning on , and ending on ,” for “during the three-year period beginning on the date of the enactment of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2002,”.Pub. L. 107–314, § 1062(a)(4)(A)December 28, 2001December 27, 2004Subsec. (a)(2)(B). , substituted “after ” for “after the end of the period specified in subparagraph (A)”.Pub. L. 107–314, § 1062(a)(4)(B)December 27, 20042001—Subsec. (a)(2). substituted “promoted—” for “promoted at a rate”, added subpar. (A), designated “not less than the rate for officers of the same armed force in the same grade and competitive category who are serving on, or have served on, the headquarters staff of their armed force; and” as subpar. (B), and inserted “after the end of the period specified in subparagraph (A), at a rate” after subpar. (B) designation.Pub. L. 107–1071996—Subsec. (b). , in first sentence, substituted “paragraphs” for “clauses” and, in second sentence, inserted “for any fiscal year” after “such objectives” and substituted “report for that fiscal year” for “periodic report required by this subsection”.Pub. L. 104–201, § 510(b), substituted “Annual Report” for “Report” in heading and “Not later than January 1 of each year, the Secretary of Defense shall submit to Congress a report on the promotion rates during the preceding fiscal year” for “The Secretary of Defense shall periodically (and not less often than every six months) report to Congress on the promotion rates” in text.Pub. L. 104–201, § 510(a)1990—Subsec. (b). substituted “the Secretary shall include in the periodic report required by this subsection information on such failure and on” for “the Secretary shall immediately notify Congress of such failure and of”.Pub. L. 101–5101988—Subsec. (a)(1), (3). inserted “to the next higher grade” after “promoted”.Pub. L. 100–456

Effective Date of 2009 Amendment, , , provided that the amendment made by section 1073(c)(2) is effective as of , and as if included in as enacted.Pub. L. 111–84, div. A, title X, § 1073(c)123 Stat. 2474Pub. L. 110–417Oct. 28, 2009Oct. 14, 2008

children.text: officers who are serving on, or have served on, the Joint Staff are expected, as a group, to be promoted to the next higher grade at a rate not less than the rate for officers of the same armed force in the same grade and competitive category who are serving on, or have served on, the headquarters staff of their armed force; and

officers in the grade of major (or in the case of the Navy, lieutenant commander) or above who have been designated as a joint qualified officer are expected, as a group, to be promoted to the next higher grade at a rate not less than the rate for all officers of the same armed force in the same grade and competitive category.
